evidence.scientific_clarity: Six-cluster KDE ridgeline clearly shows distribution shapes, relative medians, and right-tail heaviness; brief's claim (stacked KDE with cluster labels) is fully satisfied.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 set in script; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap-path) DejaVu Sans — not editable/selectable text; DejaVu Sans is the Matplotlib default.
evidence.palette: Six-color Tableau palette (#4e79a7, #f28e2b, #59a14f, #e15759, #76b7b2, #b07aa1) is colorblind-friendly; categorical use is appropriate for cluster identity; no green/red directional misuse.
evidence.layout_density: Ridges fill the panel with mild overlap (0.55); tight_layout and bbox_inches='tight' used; cluster labels on y-axis; no gapping or label collisions visible.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately matches figure; no legend needed (colors tied to y-axis labels); title present; minor issue: no explicit bw_method documentation note and svg.fonttype not set, but overall presentation is clean.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF exists and is real vector geometry, but pdffonts shows Type 3 (non-selectable, bitmapped glyph outlines) because fonttype=42 was not set in the script.
evidence.file_format: All four required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (47 KB), figure.png (48 KB), caption.tex (449 B), gen_figure.py (1.98 KB); bonus figure.svg also present.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names the kernel, bandwidth (0.25), normalization method, and provides a specific directional finding (Cluster 1 lowest median; Clusters 5–6 heavier right tails and higher medians).

overall_strengths: Scientifically effective ridgeline plot with well-chosen Tableau palette, correct KDE normalization, and informative stacking that lets the reader compare all six cluster distributions at a glance.
Caption is exemplary: it states kernel type, bandwidth, normalization, and a concrete numerical/directional finding, giving Reviewer 2 nothing to complain about on scientific content.
overall_weaknesses: Typography/font fidelity: pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0) are not set, resulting in Type 3 bitmapped glyph paths instead of TrueType — text is not selectable or editable in the PDF, and DejaVu Sans is the default fallback font rather than a deliberate choice.

No explicit rcParams configuration for font family; using Matplotlib defaults means the figure will not match a journal's preferred sans-serif (e.g., Helvetica/Arial) without additional configuration.
